economic_notes: KFN Enterprises Limited Partnership is a major business entity (99.99% owned) with callable debt of $7,380,420 guaranteed by the Nation. Nation has investments in joint arrangements through the LP. Multiple land development partnerships (Saskay, Kwosel, Kwikwetlem Holdings LP) are related entities. Real Estate Foundation of BC contributed $480,000. BC Housing provided $1,603,205 in revenue.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022, not 2024 as template default suggests. Three entities (KFN Enterprises Ltd., Choxw Holdings Corp., KFN Enterprises LP) are accounted for using modified equity method (GBEs/partnerships), not fully consolidated. Only Kwikwetlem First Nation Social Housing Program is fully consolidated. Nation has guaranteed $9,220,000 in Bank of Montreal loans for KFN Enterprises LP with $7,380,420 drawn. Loss from partnerships of $652,424 recognized in 2022 vs. $2,046,279 gain in 2021 - significant swing. Capital Fund amortization of $330,217 included in expenses. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,320,083 + BC Housing $1,603,205 + CMHC $236,679 + BC Hydro $27,647 + FNHА $129,625 + FNESC $80,124 + FN Lands Mgmt $151,643 + Fraser Health $15,000 + Govt of Canada $1,213,493 + New Relationship Trust $55,000 + Province of BC $964,835 + Real Estate Foundation $480,000 + Gaming LP $159,020 + Property tax $262,556 = approximately $7,699,310 but using segment disclosure other government = $5,088,624 + ISC $2,320,083 = $7,408,707. No remuneration schedule included in the provided text. Expenses_infrastructure includes Capital Program $23,497 + Capital Fund $330,217 = $353,714.
